prompt: We're convening a leadership steering session to set the multi-year direction and priorities for payments — purely the strategic direction and mandate, not a delivery or costing exercise. Who should be in the room?

assertions.text: States the meeting type AND which room-breadth mode it used (tight vs inclusive)
Selects TIGHT (leadership-only) mode and justifies it from the direction-only / no-deliverability framing
assertions.rationale: No investment/deliverability signal should select tight.

assertions.text: Surfaces Bram Hendriks as the single payments-line voice in the ranked list
assertions.rationale: Tight mode: senior represents the line.

assertions.text: Aisha Khan (Tribe Lead) is collapsed to a 'represented by Bram' note, NOT ranked at top altitude
assertions.rationale: Tight mode collapses the same-line delivery lead into the leader.

assertions.text: Ranked list has one voice per payments stream (no domain-architect + tribe-lead stacking)
assertions.rationale: The user's core one-voice-per-stream requirement under tight mode.

assertions.text: Flags the unprofiled senior/escalation chain (Mark van Dijk / Jeroen Meijer / Miguel Santos) in the gap note as the likely chair/sign-off
assertions.rationale: Strategy altitude lives in the unprofiled chain.

assertions.text: Every ranked stakeholder has a 1-10 score and rationale, ordered; no fabricated people in the ranked list
